Name: Arachedron
Description: Arachedrons were small creatures which attacked the bounty hunter Boba Fett aboard the Reverie.[1]

Name: Babasta beast
Description: A babasta beast was a creature. They could be served boiled, where it was a speciality dish at The Broken Tusk on Reuss VIII.[1]

Name: Bacteria
Description: Bacteria were organisms whose resilience allowed them to survive on inhospitable worlds such as Bastatha.[2] Some bacteria could cause infections. To prevent those during a surgical procedure, bio-sterilization fields were employed.[3]

Name: Bageraset
Description: Bagerasets were a type of creature that lived in the galaxy.[1]

Name: Begamor shrimp
Description: The translation in this article's title is conjectural.

Name: Bladeback boar
Description: A bladeback boar was a type of creature.[1]

Name: Blash'narl
Description: A blash'narl was a creature that exhibited feeding frenzies.[1]

Name: Blaxorn
Description: A blaxorn was a creature with spots.[1]

Name: Boga
Description: The boga was a creature. "Male boga" appeared on an Aurebesh list of animal sounds recorded by the clone trooper Tech. During the Mission to Skako Minor, Tech scrolled through the list and used a recording he took earlier to summon several keeradaks to escape on.[1]

Name: Boohar cat
Description: The anglicization and translation in this article's title is conjectural.

Name: Boohar pitbull
Description: The anglicization and translation in this article's title is conjectural.

Name: Borcatu
Description: Borcatus were a type of creatures that was found in the galaxy.[1]

Name: Bramalish
Description: Bramalish were a species that resided on the planet Karaxis.[1]

Name: Bravaisian precursor species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Caniphant
Description: A caniphant was a four-legged creature with a tapered snout. They grew to lengths of 1.2 meters long, and stood .75 meters high. They were employed as guard animals. Ellam kept three in a force cage in his speeder lot.

Name: Carsung
Description: A carsung was a creature. They could be tamed so that individuals were capable of riding them.

Name: Chimaera
Description: The chimaera was a three-headed monstrous[1] legendary creature[2] known to the Unknown Regions Paccosh species and represented with arc-like spider legs, a cluster of small snakes resembling stalked eyes rising from the center of the arcs, and two much larger serpentine heads perched atop long barbed necks curving up and around. The large snakes were described as having their heads and open mouths pointing defiantly

Name: Coney
Description: Coneys were creatures that could be easily frightened. General Naem Yrros likened the Rhamalians to frightened coneys due to their insistence on not relying on any forms of modern technology.[1]

Name: Cow
Description: A cow was a species of creature that could be milked, similar to a nerf.[1] Kaadu resembled tall two-legged cows.[2] On some occasions when Chef Strono Tuggs stationed his traveling restaurant at Docking Bay 7 Food and Cargo in Batuu's Black Spire Outpost, he served Batuuan Beef Pot Roast produced by Batuuan cows that grazed Naboo's grasslands.[3]

Name: Crocodile
Description: A crocodile was creature in the galaxy. The grakkt, a species native to the planet Naboo, had a head that resembled a crocodile and the body of an armored lion.[1] Likkas, a species from the planet Abonshee, resembled long-legged crocodiles.[2]

Name: Crunchers
Description: Crunchers were rodent-like creatures that lived on the planet Jeosyn during the Galactic Civil War. The Dreamers, who named the creatures, used them to dispose of stormtrooper corpses. Iden Versio deemed the practice ghoulish.[1]

Name: Dalef razorback
Description: A Dalef razorback was a large creature. It was about 200 kilograms of muscle and fangs. One specimen was in the Imperial zoo where it gave the impression of being a lazy overgrown house pet.

Name: Dask
Description: Dasks were creatures that could be domesticated and ridden. The Ebranite Rith Tar'ak was adept at riding dasks.

Name: Derbit
Description: Derbits were a creature found in the galaxy.[1]

Name: Diplopod
Description: Diplopods were created by author Jason Fry as placeholder creatures for the novel The Weapon of a Jedi: A Luke Skywalker Adventure, destined to eventually be replaced by happabores as a tie-in to Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens. Having grown attached to the creatures, Fry chose to reuse the idea for Servants of the Empire: The Secret Academy.[2]

Name: Drydak
Description: A drydak was a creature.[2] The word was sometimes used to insult people, such as "stupid drydaks like you"[3] or "you stubborn drydak."[1] According to Tey Sirrek, the resemblance between Vildar Mac and a drydak was uncanny.[4]

Name: Fairy
Description: Fairies were small creatures that could be compared to Wisties in appearance.[1]

Name: Flycatcher
Description: Flycatchers were creatures native to the planet Wroona. They looked like large spherical rocks with long adhesive tongues and four large webbed hands tipped with claws. They fed on avians.

Name: Freiloid
Description: A Freiloid was a creature.

Name: Fyrnock
Description: Fyrnocks were silicon-based[5] creatures originally indigenous to the jungles of the planet Anaxes. Surviving the destruction of their homeworld, many fyrnocks inhabited the resulting Anaxes asteroid belt, including the asteroid PM-1203, which contained Fort Anaxes, a military base used by the Grand Army of the Republic during the Clone Wars. Fyrnocks were hurt by sunlight and could only live under the cover of darkn

Name: Gheriol
Description: Gheriols appeared in The Acolyte: Wayseeker, a 2025 novel written by Justina Ireland.[1]

Name: Gizka
Description: The gizka existed in the galaxy. The Six-Card Gizka Limit game shared the name.[1]

Name: Gloomling
Description: A Glooming was a creature mentioned by Marlaa Jinara on the planet Tenoo that haunt people's homes until they leave so that they can take over. Being as legends and rumors that they did not exist, they were said to have glowing eyes that can see in the dark but they don't like at bright lights, giving them a reason why they came out only at the night.[1]

Name: Gorlap
Description: Gorlaps were creatures that produced eggs that were known as gorlap eggs. They were referred to in the phrase "last one (…) is a rotten gorlap egg," which was used to urge people to get to a place swiftly, e.g. "Last one to it is a rotten gorlap egg!" said by Maz Kanata to Kai Brightstar and Marlaa Jinara; "Last one there is a rotten gorlap egg!" said by Jinara to Kanata and Brightstar;[1] and "Last one to the dig si

Name: Greeper
Description: A greeper was a creature. It was mouse-like.

Name: Gutterguppy
Description: A gutterguppy was a type of creature.[1]

Name: Ikadrian flitter-whipit
Description: The Ikadrian flitter-whipit was a creature known for its short attention span. According to the RA-7 protocol droid DK-RA-43 in the book the Galactic Explorer's Guide, his master, Hondo Ohnaka, had an attention span shorter than some Ikadrian flitter-whipits.[1]

Name: Kabus-Dabeh Monstrosity
Description: The Kabus-Dabeh Monstrosity was a creature that resided in the ruins of Kabus-Dabeh on the planet Muzara. It had four tentacles that were tipped with poisonous barbs, a fang-filled maw and a lumpy tail.

Name: Kethriak marauder
Description: A Kethriak marauder was a wild creature that could also be kept in captivity in zoos.

Name: Kivaroa
Description: Kivaroa were created for the 2015 canon short story "Bottleneck" by John Jackson Miller, included in The Rise of the Empire bind-up.

Name: Krag
Description: A krag was a creature in the galaxy. The expletive "krag wrangler" was used as an insult.[1]

Name: Lake-diver
Description: Lake-divers were creatures found on the world of Pondakree. They were particularly easy to capture, and when Pondakree native Emarr Ottkreg witnessed the Galactic Empire destroy several Alliance to Restore the Republic starships with ease at the Battle of Endor he compared difficulty to catching lake-divers.[1]

Name: Lion
Description: Lions were a species of leonids whose face served as the Mandalorian Clan Eldar's signet. They were known for representing concepts such as bravery, courage, and pride.[2]

Name: Loreek
Description: The loreek was a creature with the ability to sing. During the battle on Bahlah, the leader of the Morubas Crime Family, Jjan exclaimed that the loreek were singing when the Shadow Collective attempted to break down the doors to his family's stronghold.[1]

Name: Louse
Description: Lice were a creature found throughout the galaxy.[2]

Name: Lucrusian fengrill
Description: Lucrusian fengrills were a type of creature. To be as "drunk as a Lucrusian fengrill" was a saying that could refer to an inebriated individual.

Name: Luftgriff
Description: Luftgriffs were creatures on Vandor that flocked together. The Luftgriff Peak shared its name with the creatures. The owner of The Lodge, Tibbs Ospe, once threatened to feed the smuggler Lando Calrissian to luftgriffs for skipping out on his tab, but following the raid on Kessel, Calrissian returned and paid his debts.[1]

Name: Manpha-fowl
Description: Manpha-fowl was a type of creature[1] that shared its name with the planet Manpha.[2]

Name: Mantis
Description: A mantis was a lifeform that did not possess a stinger. It shared its name with the S-161 "Stinger" XL luxury yacht Stinger Mantis owned by Greez Dritus, which he named such because he liked the image it provoked.[1] The insectoid Yam'rii species[2] were praying mantis-like.[3]

Name: Marsh-slither
Description: A marsh-slither was a creature native to the planet where the Alliance base DVL-181 was located. Marsh-slithers were known to get into the landing gear of starships on the base.[1]

Name: Mooka
Description: The mooka were a chimerical[1] creature found in the galaxy.[2]

Name: Morag's beast
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Mudhen
Description: A mudhen was a creature known to the miners of the planet Mimban.

Name: Nanak
Description: Nanaks were a type of creature native to the planet Egips that could communicate with sentient species, like humans. Gherd was a nanak owned by Harra the Hutt.[1]

Name: Nightscowl
Description: Nightscowls were forest-dwelling creatures native to Okator VIII. They were considered dangerous.

Name: Oyster
Description: The oyster was an organism in the galaxy that was known to have a soft navel. Skoova Stev was accused of having gone as soft as an oyster's navel by Wan'yek Whett after Skoova didn't use his harpoon to kill a gang of pirate Quarren.[1]

Name: Pamaradian prancer
Description: A Pamaradian prancer was a creature that lived in the galaxy. After the death of her sister Paige during the Resistance's evacuation of D'Qar, Rose Tico thought of how the pair would never be able to get to ride a Pamaradian prancer.[1]

Name: Phindian Zillik
Description: The Phindian Zillik was a species native to Phindar with scales that were highly resistant to a broad spectrum of solar radiation.

Name: Pit-boar
Description: A pit-boar was a creature whose teeth decorated the half-mask worn by Lando Calrissian while in disguise as a skiff guard in Jabba's Palace during the rescue attempt of Han Solo in 3 ABY.[1]

Name: Po'ackster
Description: Po'acksters were creatures with four eyes that produced pink milk.[1] At some point between 3 ABY and 4 ABY,[2] they were considered to be rare and highly sought after, and a number of them were kept within an Imperial warehouse in the Imperial Warehousing District on[1] the planet[3] E'ronoh. During this time, the Weequay Jand led the smuggler Sana Starros to the warehouse as she was in need of a quick score. After

Name: Pucksy
Description: A pucksy first appeared in "The Helpful Harvester," the first part of the fourteenth episode of the second season of Star Wars: Young Jedi Adventures animated series[1] released on March 19, 2025.[2]

Name: Rexx-boar
Description: Rexx-boars were a kind of animal found on Grizal.[1]

Name: Rodir
Description: Boba Fett? Boba Fett? Where?

Name: Rokur Gepta's pet food
Description: Rokur Gepta fed his pets a certain kind of animal for their food. They were very much like mice, with pelts of curly golden fur, large blue eyes, and bushy tails. They were sleek, clean, and radiated warmth, and let out squeaks when in pain or distressed. They made Gepta shudder, and he fed them to his pets alive.

Name: Rondat
Description: A Rondat is a animal native to Kuan. When Captain Trace was reciting a proverb about curiosity killing some creature, Maarek Stele mentioned a rondat, to which Trace suggested the rondat before dismissing him.

Name: Ropnid
Description: A ropnid was a creature. They were known for having an unpleasant underbelly.[1]

Name: Rork
Description: A rork was a creature. They organized themselves in packs.[1]

Name: Saphantine
Description: Saphantine was a creature that resided on Taltos.[1] By 382 BBY,[2] Padawan Silandra Sho got stuck in the burrow where a Saphantine resided.[1]

Name: Selliwyrm
Description: A selliwyrm was a creature known to the Velabri. They were considered to be cowardly.[1]

Name: Sharrapon
Description: Sharrapons were creatures that lived in the galaxy. Landonis Balthazar Calrissian stole Batalla's sharrapon during the Calladus festival and raised the creature.[1]

Name: Sheep
Description: Sheep were a herd species. One variety was the cochukka, which were kept by shepherds on the planet Pkori.[3] In 231 BBY,[4] Eye of the Nihil Marchion Ro referred to the followers of Supreme Chancellor Lina Soh as her sheep.[5] In 22 BBY,[6] while on a mission to the planet Thule, the Jedi Knight Iskat Akaris noted that the local slugs feeding on the stone of a canyon wall looked like a herd of sheep grazing on a ver

Name: Sithspawn
Description: Sithspawn[2] were creatures affiliated with the Sith.[3] The Jedi Temple Detention Center in the Jedi Temple on Coruscant was initially built to detain them.[2] After the crime lord Cenevax captured Dooku and his Padawan Qui-Gon Jinn, she was attacked by a convor Dooku had brought with him named Calleen and lashed out, calling her "Sith spawn", before killing the convor.[1] Jedi Master Mace Windu called the assassin

Name: Skiddo
Description: These creatures first appeared in the 1987 Japanese Star Wars video game for the Famicom home console.[1] Their name was revealed in the Japanese language guide of the game serialized in Family Computer Magazine, in which it was rendered as スキッド (Sukiddo) in katakana syllabary.[2]

Name: Slime-bug
Description: A slime-bug was a creature native to the planet where the Alliance base DVL-181 was located. Slime-bugs were known to get into dinners made on the base.[1]

Name: Snitmouse
Description: A snitmouse was a type of mouse.[1]

Name: Specimen Nine
Description: Specimen 9 was a creature surgically cobbled together from a multitude of beings created by cryptosurgeons.[1]

Name: Spider-roach
Description: A spider-roach was a type of creature. After General Vukorah of the Unbroken Clan, who was holding employees of De'Rruyet Industries hostage on the[1] planet[2] Midarr, discovered that Doctor Chelli Lona Aphra had survived her attempt to kill her[1] on the planet Dol'har Hyde,[3] she remarked to Aphra that she was harder to kill than a spider-roach.[1]

Name: Squawkin
Description: A squawkin was a type of creature. The Chagrian Nihil Jeni Wataro called the Mon Calamari Chief of Security for the space station Starlight Beacon Ghal Tarpfen such a creature after she refused to allow any Nihil pirates to escape from a starship via numerous escape pods.[1]

Name: Strostodon
Description: Strostodons were large birdlike creatures found on the planet Vexos. They walked on two legs and stood about nine feet tall, flightless but able to jump and glide. Strostodons hunted in packs, and emitted loud screeches from their beaks when attacking, or trills when pacified.[1]

Name: Tanarian vulk
Description: The Tanarian vulk was a big, repugnant creature that was also was slow and plodding.[1]

Name: Tapir
Description: Tapirs were creatures with snouts. The Rodian Greedo was described as having a tapir-like snout.[1]

Name: Terazod
Description: The terazod was a creature whose meat could be cooked in the form of a stew.[1]

Name: Terrorvore
Description: Terrorvores were creatures, some of which were in the possession of King Prana.[1]

Name: Thalassian frezhin
Description: A Thalassian frezhin was a creature.

Name: The Caller in the Dark
Description: The Caller in the Dark appears in Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Eternal Throne as a bonus boss during the "Destroyer Of Worlds" Uprising.

Name: Thesselbeast
Description: A thesselbeast was a type of creature. A thesselbeast's foot was a lucky charm; one was carried by the smuggler Roark Garnet.[1]

Name: Tiehn slug
Description: The Tiehn slug was creature that was a delicacy for the Hutts.

Name: Toorga
Description: A Toorga was a type of creature that could be kept inside a Toorgash range. A Coruscant holding facility that was used by the Galactic Republic during the Clone Wars had a Toorgash range with live Toorgas located inside it.

Name: Trahio
Description: The trahio was a species of creature know to be a techivore, like the mynock and the raen sovra.[1]

Name: Tran Mariel runyip
Description: The Tran Mariel runyip was a subspecies of Runyip from Tran Mariel.[2] Like their cousins found on Yavin 4,[4] the Tran Mariel runyip had possessed hooves and tapered ears[2] but instead of a long snout[4] they possessed a long trunk and a shaggy coat of hair.[2] By 10 BBY,[5] the gangster Dryden Vos kept a Tran Mariel runyip in suspended animation in his study aboard his yacht, along with other rare animals.[1]

Name: Trooshti
Description: A trooshti was a creature that flew in swarms. According to a legend known by the Hassarian Vaclav, Jabba the Hutt had once faced a trooshti swarm.[1]

Name: Unidentified Benglor creature
Description: During the Clone Wars in 22 BBY,[2] Jedi General Anakin Skywalker and clone troopers of the 501st Legion encountered one of these creatures during a mission to Benglor. The creature killed one of Skywalker's men, but was eventually pacified by the Jedi and his second-in-command, Clone Captain CT-7567 "Rex."[1]

Name: Unidentified Charissia creature
Description: A creature that had black eyes, and had gray skin, and was native to the moon Charissia.[1]

Name: Unidentified creature (AG-37)
Description: A quadripedal species of creature with purple skin, a hunched back, plenty of sharp teeth, and at least one tusk existed in the galaxy. In the year 138 ABY, one of them was transported to the planet Iego by the delivery crew of AG-37 and Sauk, aboard AG-37's freighter.

Name: Unidentified creature (Christophsis)
Description: A creature with orange-colored skin and yellow crystals protruding from their body was native to the planet Christophsis.[1] Around 3 ABY,[2] one such creature attacked the humans Luke Skywalker and Gretta while they cruised along the surface of the planet.[1]

Name: Unidentified creature (Dallenor)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified creature (Garazeb Orrelios)
Description: This creature existed in the galaxy. Before the year 4 BBY one of these creatures attacked a friend of Garazeb Orrelios in a underground cave.[1]

Name: Unidentified creature (Hubin)
Description: This creature first appeared in Star Wars (2015) 56[1] written by Kieron Gillen, illustrated by Andrea Broccardo, and published by Marvel Comics on November 7, 2018.[4]

Name: Unidentified creature (Keeper's World)
Description: Unidentified creatures, humanoid in appearance with pink fur, dwelled on Keeper's World and were a threat to any who landed on the planet.

Name: Unidentified creature (Ronto Roasters)
Description: The species first appeared as a set decoration at the real-world Ronto Roasters within the canonical themed-attraction Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge,[1] which was first open publicly on May 31, 2019.[2]

Name: Unidentified creature (Solace)
Description: The creatures were the size of small Loth-wolves with hard, chitinous plating and long snouts. There mandibles could discharged a strange energy that could disabled a person with a small touch.[1]

Name: Unidentified furry humanoid creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified Galgus creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified Galjaero creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified GUHL-J03870 creature
Description: A creature native to the planet GUHL-J03870 had purple or pink fur. One of these creatures came aboard one of the AAL-1971/9.1 Troop Transports when it landed on GUHL-J03870.[1]

Name: Unidentified long-necked Irff creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified long-nosed Irff creature
Description: A green and blue skinned creature was native to the planet Irff. According to Jek Tono Porkins, they were known to be delicious.[1]

Name: Unidentified mythosaur
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified night creature
Description: A creature was native to the planet Vyndal. A woman said to Kanan Jarrus that if he remained in the forest he would have been killed by these creatures.[1]

Name: Unidentified Noaxson creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified Odona creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified pack beast
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified reek-like creature
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified six-legged alien
Description: A six-legged alien species existed in the galaxy. They were identified by big, black eyes, six legs that possessed claws, large ears, spines on its back, and antennae. They were apparently popular enough to be kept as pets.

Name: Unidentified two-headed rodent
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified winged creature
Description: In 4 ABY[3] two of these creatures were kept in a cave adiacent to the main pit that contained Jabba's rancor, below Jabba's Palace on Tatooine.[2]

Name: Valath
Description: A valath was a huge and deadly creature that was the apex predator of the planet Chandar's Folly. It had a hide that boasted unique chromatophores which allowed it to blend into its background. Whilst being hunted by Governor Wilhuff Tarkin and nineteen other hunters, Darth Vader killed a valath, without the use of his lightsaber, and wore its skin as a cloak.[1]

Name: Wentoc
Description: A wentoc was a type of creature that lived in the galaxy. It lent its name to the phrase "jran and wentoc."[1]

Name: Whale
Description: Whales were a type of creature that existed in various locations across the galaxy; which included the Cartusion whale,[1] the ruhau-whale,[2] and the oceanic starwhale species.[3] The cyborg creatures that formed the whale-ships,[4] and the space-faring purrgil were also considered whale-like species.[5] Additionally, the aiwhas—non-sentient species[6] of winged cetaceans native to the planet Kamino[7]—were also cal

Name: Wharlithan
Description: Wharlithans were a species of horned creatures. Their horns were used in a musical instrument by the Lanai on Ahch-To.[1]

Name: Whelorian snake-eater
Description: The Whelorian snake-eater was a creature noted for its narrow, pinched features. Luke Skywalker once likened General Sebastian Parnell to a Whelorian snake-eater.

Name: Wyrm
Description: Wyrms were creatures found on Merakai. An ancient Sith wyrm inhabited Yavin 4, but was destroyed by Exar Kun around 4000 BBY.

Name: Ysalamiri
Description: Ysalamiri first appeared in Star Wars canon as sculptures in the 2017 Star Wars Rebels episode "Through Imperial Eyes,"[2] the seventeenth episode of the show's third season.[4] The creature's name was first identified in the Behind-the-Scenes Gallery of the episode guide that accompanied "Through Imperial Eyes" on StarWars.com.[1] They first appeared in the Star Wars Legends continuity in the 1991 novel Heir to the

Name: Zaproach
Description: A zaproach was a creature that lived in a nest. To "stir up a zaproach nest" was a saying that related to causing trouble.

Name: Zare Leonis's diplopod
Description: A diplopod was used by Zare Leonis during training exercises at the officer's Academy on Arkanis. Like other diplopods, it was insect-like and had a stinger, as well as a green shell and innards that Leonis found slightly nauseating. It seemed to respond well to Leonis's commands and Leonis was pleased that he only rarely had to use his provided electro-prod on it, as he found it distasteful. During the exercise, Leo

Name: "Mynock"
Description: Redirect to:

Name: "Rancor" Johans
Description: Redirect to:

Name: "Wampa"
Description: The title of this article is a nickname, call sign, or alias.

Name: AT-AT Swimmer (Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire)
Description: The version used by Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire could operate underwater and had shields. It included at least 6 laser cannons, including a rear double laser cannon to stem attacks from the rear, and was fitted with a launch bay containing at least five Shark underwater fighters. The new design looked similar to the old one, although sleeker in design. The command bridge was also replaced by another similar to a st

Name: A Boy and His Monster
Description: Redirect to:

Name: A Boy and His Monster: The Rancor Keeper's Tale
Description: When the fearsome rancor monster is found in a shipwreck in Tatooine's Dune Sea, Jabba's underlings present it as a gift to the gangster. Jabba tasks Malakili with the care and feeding of his new pet. Malakili is overjoyed to have custody of Jabba's rancor, but he resents the captivity of what he considers so magnificent a beast. Over time, he and the rancor develop a certain rapport, which convinces Malakili that he

Name: A Creature in the Stars
Description: Redirect to:

Name: A Creature in the Stars!
Description: Over here! Help! Please, help!
